title: Research Hardware
intro: A look inside the SEAL lab — robots, sensors, and experiments built by the team over the years.
From autonomous pipe inspection robots to microfabricated sensors and novel solid-state cooling technology, these photos capture some of the hardware that has come out of the lab.

images.title: pipe-inspection-robot-1
images.description: SEAL's autonomous pipe inspection robot traversing a test track

images.title: pipe-inspection-robot-2
images.description: Pipe inspection robot gripping a pipe in the lab

images.title: cable-specimen
images.description: High-voltage cable test specimen used in power quality research

images.title: microsensor-sem
images.description: Scanning electron microscope image of a microfabricated sensor structure

images.title: efa-cooling-jets
images.description: Electrostatic Fluid Acceleration (EFA) air jets — solid-state cooling technology developed at SEAL

images.title: moisture-cookie-test
images.description: A cookie placed on a fringing electric field sensor array — moisture sensing research can measure food freshness non-invasively

images.title: handheld-sensor-pear
images.description: Handheld fringing electric field sensor scanning a pear — demonstrating portable non-contact sensing
